summary: Iteration 12 — verified 7 new P2/P3 features + frontend public TrackOrder page + variant Copy/WhatsApp share buttons. Created /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12_p2_p3.py (16 tests, ALL PASS). Regression: re-ran iter_9 (33) + iter_10 (9) + iter_11 (9) = 51/51 PASS. Combined 67/67 backend tests passing. Frontend: /track page renders form, valid order shows result, invalid shows error; family detail page renders 27 copy + 27 share buttons with correct data-testid and the share anchor has https://wa.me/?text= href as required.
action_items: No blockers. All 7 features verified end-to-end. Main agent can ship.
critical_code_review_comments: backend/server.py is now 1505 lines — recommend extracting public endpoints (public/track, public/my-quotes, public/quote/{qid}/submit-po, _public_order_summary, _notify_admin_po_received, _ack_customer_po_received) into a new routers/public.py module. Single file is becoming a regression hotspot; the very NameError on `public_shipments` that was just fixed is symptomatic of inline edits to a too-long file.

routers/auth.py brute-force lockout uses module-level dicts (_failed_attempts, _lockouts). It works correctly per-process and is cleared on restart (acceptable for preview). For multi-worker production, this WILL behave unevenly across workers — recommend a Redis or DB-backed implementation when moving beyond single-worker uvicorn. Not blocking for current scope.

_ack_customer_po_received correctly wraps both SMTP and WhatsApp sends in try/except and returns boolean ok flags — submit-po will still return 200 even when ack fails. Verified by code inspection (lines 909-979). Aligns with the 'best-effort, never raises' contract documented in the docstring.

/public/track endpoint correctly normalises the path via .replace('%2F', '/').strip().strip('/'); however the explicit `%2F` replacement is technically redundant since FastAPI's :path converter already decodes percent-encoding. Harmless but slightly misleading — caller may think double-encoding is supported when it isn't.

/health/integrations does a real SMTP_SSL.login() round-trip per call — at ~1-2s per check this is fine for an admin-triggered health probe but should NOT be wired into a polling dashboard or automated heartbeat. Document this in the UI to avoid accidental DoS on the SMTP provider.

Diff endpoint /quotations/{qid}/diff/{other_qid} — implementation correctly compares by product_code key. Edge case: if two lines in the same quote share a product_code, only one will be tracked. Not common but worth a note in the API doc.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on the new surfaces — /track form/result/error + family-detail copy/share buttons all render with correct testids and the share anchor has the wa.me/?text= href.

test_credentials: admin@hrexporter.com / Admin@123 (intact). The brute-force lockout test used a synthetic email lockouttest_iter12_<uuid>@example.com so the real admin account was never locked. The 'admin counter clears on success' test deliberately did 2 wrong logins (under the 5-attempt threshold) for admin then a successful login to verify the counter resets — admin remained unlocked throughout.
seed_data_creation: None persistent. Iteration 9 regression creates a TEST_ITERATION9 quote + order for Harsh Gujarati but cleans up in fixture teardown.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run iter_12: `pytest /app/backend/tests/test_iteration12_p2_p3.py -v` (16 tests, ~8s). Full regression: `pytest backend/tests/test_iteration9_phase123.py backend/tests/test_iteration10_testmode_outbound.py backend/tests/test_iteration11_password_length.py backend/tests/test_iteration12_p2_p3.py -v` (67 tests, ~40s). FRONTEND_URL = REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL from /app/frontend/.env (currently https://quotation-system-20.preview.emergentagent.com). Note: brute-force lockout is per-process in-memory — if a previous run left admin locked, restart backend: `sudo supervisorctl restart backend`. The test_5_fails_then_429 test uses a unique synthetic email each invocation so it is safe to re-run repeatedly.
rca of the issue: N/A — no bugs found this iteration. The pre-existing NameError on `public_shipments` in _public_order_summary that main agent flagged is fixed (verified by code read at lines 692-706 — local var is constructed inline before use) and the public /track endpoint exercises this code path successfully in test_track_correct_phone_verified.
